originalTitle: 3 Reasons to Avoid PFBC and 1 Stock to Buy Instead
summary: Since November 2025, Preferred Bank has been in a holding pattern, posting a small return of 1.3% while floating around $94.09. The stock also fell short of the S&P 500’s 11.3% gain during that period.
content:
Since November 2025, Preferred Bank has been in a holding pattern, posting a small return of 1.3% while floating around $94.09. The stock also fell short of the S&P 500’s 11.3% gain during that period.

Is now the time to buy Preferred Bank, or should you be careful about including it in your portfolio? Dive into our full research report to see our analyst team’s opinion, it’s free .

Why Is Preferred Bank Not Exciting?

We're swiping left on Preferred Bank for now. Here are three reasons you should be careful with PFBC and a stock we'd rather own.

1. Net Interest Income Points to Soft Demand

While bank generate revenue from multiple sources, investors view net interest income as a cornerstone - its predictable, recurring characteristics stand in sharp contrast to the volatility of one-time fees.

Preferred Bank’s net interest income has grown at a 9% annualized rate over the last five years, slightly worse than the broader banking industry and in line with its total revenue.

Net interest margin (NIM) represents how much a bank earns in relation to its outstanding loans. It's one of the most important metrics to track because it shows how a bank's loans are performing and whether it has the ability to command higher premiums for its services.

Over the past two years, Preferred Bank’s net interest margin averaged 3.8%. However, its margin contracted by 61.7 basis points (100 basis points = 1 percentage point) over that period.

This decline was a headwind for its net interest income. While prevailing rates are a major determinant of net interest margin changes over time, the decline could mean Preferred Bank either faced competition for loans and deposits or experienced a negative mix shift in its balance sheet composition.

3. Recent EPS Growth Below Our Standards

While long-term earnings trends give us the big picture, we also track EPS over a shorter period because it can provide insight into an emerging theme or development for the business.

Preferred Bank’s weak 1.3% annual EPS growth over the last two years aligns with its revenue trend. On the bright side, this tells us its incremental sales were profitable.

Preferred Bank isn’t a terrible business, but it isn’t one of our picks. With its shares underperforming the market lately, the stock trades at 1.3× forward P/B (or $94.09 per share). While this valuation is fair, the upside isn’t great compared to the potential downside. We're pretty confident there are more exciting stocks to buy at the moment. We’d suggest looking at a fast-growing restaurant franchise with an A+ ranch dressing sauce .

High-Quality Stocks for All Market Conditions

ONE MORE THING: Top 5 Growth Stocks. The biggest stock winners almost always had one thing in common before they ran. Revenue growing like crazy. Meta. CrowdStrike. Broadcom. Our AI flagged all three. They returned 315%, 314%, and 455%, respectively.

Find out which 5 stocks it's flagging for this month - FREE. Get Our Top 5 Growth Stocks for Free HERE .

Stocks that have made our list include now familiar names such as Nvidia (+1,326% between June 2020 and June 2025) as well as under-the-radar businesses like the once-small-cap company Comfort Systems (+782% five-year return). Find your next big winner with StockStory today .
chineseTitle: 避免 PFBC 的 3 个理由和值得购买的 1 只股票
chineseSummary: 自 2025 年 11 月以来，Preferred Bank 一直处于持有模式，回报率为 1.3%，徘徊在 94.09 美元左右。该股同期涨幅也低于标准普尔 500 指数 11.3% 的涨幅。
chineseContent:
自 2025 年 11 月以来，Preferred Bank 一直处于持有模式，回报率为 1.3%，徘徊在 94.09 美元左右。该股同期涨幅也低于标准普尔 500 指数 11.3% 的涨幅。

现在是购买 Preferred Bank 的好时机，还是应该谨慎地将其纳入您的投资组合？深入研究我们的完整研究报告，了解我们分析师团队的意见，它是免费的。

为什么优先银行不令人兴奋？

我们现在在 Preferred Bank 上向左滑动。以下是您应该谨慎对待 PFBC 和我们宁愿持有的股票的三个原因。

1.净利息收入表明需求疲软

虽然银行的收入来自多种来源，但投资者将净利息收入视为基石——其可预测、经常性的特征与一次性费用的波动性形成鲜明对比。

过去五年，优先银行的净利息收入以年化 9% 的速度增长，略差于整个银行业，但与其总收入保持一致。

净息差（NIM）代表银行与其未偿贷款相关的收入。它是需要跟踪的最重要的指标之一，因为它显示了银行的贷款表现如何以及是否有能力为其服务收取更高的保费。

过去两年，优先银行的平均净息差为3.8%。然而，其利润率在此期间收缩了 61.7 个基点（100 个基点 = 1 个百分点）。

这种下降对其净利息收入构成了不利影响。虽然现行利率是净息差随时间变化的主要决定因素，但利率下降可能意味着优先银行要么面临贷款和存款的竞争，要么经历资产负债表构成的负面组合变化。

3.近期每股收益增长低于我们的标准

虽然长期盈利趋势让我们了解全局，但我们还跟踪较短时期内的每股收益，因为它可以提供对新兴主题或业务发展的洞察。

过去两年，优先银行每股收益年增长率仅为 1.3%，这与其收入趋势相符。从好的方面来说，这告诉我们其增量销售是有利可图的。

优先银行并不是一家糟糕的公司，但它不是我们的选择之一。由于其股价最近表现不佳，该股的市盈率为 1.3 倍（即每股 94.09 美元）。虽然这一估值是公平的，但与潜在的下行空间相比，上行空间并不大。我们非常有信心目前有更多令人兴奋的股票可供购买。我们建议寻找一家快速发展的特许经营餐厅，配备 A+ 牧场调味酱。

适合所有市场条件的优质股票

还有一件事：前 5 名成长型股票。最大的股票赢家在逃跑之前几乎总是有一个共同点。收入疯狂增长。元。群众罢工。博通。我们的人工智能标记了这三个。他们的回报率分别为 315%、314% 和 455%。

免费找出本月标记的 5 只股票。在此免费获取我们排名前 5 的成长型股票。

进入我们名单的股票包括现在熟悉的名字，如 Nvidia（2020 年 6 月至 2025 年 6 月期间增长 1,326%），以及曾经不为人知的企业，如曾经的小盘股公司 Comfort Systems（五年回报率增长 782%）。立即通过 StockStory 寻找您的下一个大赢家。
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于财经市场信号，适合用于观察利率、汇率、股债商品、金融机构和宏观政策变化。
